title: MCP proxy closes on malformed downstream JSON-RPC responses
description: Vibe finding: internal/adapters/mcpproxy.go currently calls decodeJSONRPC on downstream payloads and returns error on decode failure, which terminates the proxy instead of logging and forwarding malformed downstream responses as-is per specs/technical_v1.1.md §11.2. Add regression coverage for malformed downstream envelopes and invalid frame handling.

title: Codex shell execution diverges from fuse run runtime semantics
description: Vibe finding: internal/adapters/codexshell.go executes via executeCapturedShellCommand instead of the same job-control path as fuse run. Interactive/signal-sensitive commands miss process-group setup, foreground TTY handoff, and signal forwarding required by specs/technical_v1.1.md §10.1 and §12.2.

title: MCP oversize frame rejection can hang the proxy
description: In internal/adapters/mcpproxy.go, proxyAgentToDownstream handles oversized agent frames by calling drainOversizeFrame(reader, contentLength). Because contentLength is attacker-controlled and there is no timeout or bounded discard, a client can send a huge Content-Length and then stop sending bytes, leaving the proxy blocked in io.CopyN forever instead of promptly rejecting the frame. This is a DoS/security regression in the new oversize handling path introduced for spec compliance. Add bounded discard/connection abort behavior and tests.

title: Residual v1 spec conformance pass
description: Implement the remaining gaps after the initial technical_v1.1 landing: expand doctor/live diagnostics, finish MCP proxy frame and protocol edge handling, unify Codex-shell execution with run-mode behavior, and honor config-driven event pruning with tests.

title: Remediate technical_v1.1 implementation gaps
description: Implement the approved remediation batch for spec divergences in classification, MCP approval/proxy behavior, run-mode execution, approval state, tests, and docs.

title: CLI conformance gaps remain in fuse run and Codex config handling
description: Vibe finding: fuse run usage errors still exit through root Execute() with status 1 instead of the spec-required status 2, and install/uninstall codex only manage CODEX_HOME or ~/.codex/config.toml instead of also handling repo-local .codex/config.toml as allowed by specs/technical_v1.1.md §3.2 and §4.2.

title: doctor passes Claude hook setups that still violate the required matcher/timeout contract
description: internal/cli/doctor.go uses hasFuseHook() to mark Claude integration as PASS if any PreToolUse hook command equals 'fuse hook evaluate'. It does not verify the required Bash and mcp__.* matchers or the 30s timeout, even though doctor is supposed to validate the actual hook schema. doctor can therefore greenlight a broken Claude setup that still misses MCP mediation or the required timeout. Add schema-aware validation and tests.

title: doctor --live reports terminal checks without actually validating them
description: internal/cli/doctor.go marks raw-mode and foreground handoff as PASS based on stdin TIOCGPGRP access, and checkLiveForegroundProcessGroup calls ForegroundChildProcessGroupIfTTY(os.Getpid()), which is not a real child-process handoff probe. doctor may falsely report setup as healthy or fail spuriously depending on process-group layout. Implement real /dev/tty raw-mode enter/restore and a safe foreground handoff viability probe, then add tests.

title: Enforce disable marker + event logging + cleanup + config wiring
description:
Combined task for all hook.go/runner.go enhancements (file conflict prevents parallel work).

## 1. Disable Marker Enforcement
Add to internal/config/paths.go:
- EnabledMarkerPath() string — returns filepath.Join(StateDir(), "enabled")
- IsDisabled() bool — returns true when marker file does NOT exist

In hook.go runHookInternal(), add at top before JSON parsing:
  if config.IsDisabled() { return 0 }

In runner.go ExecuteCommand(), add before classification:
  if config.IsDisabled() { return executeShellCommand(command, cwd) }

## 2. Best-Effort Event Logging in Hook Path
Add logHookEvent(req, result) helper to hook.go.
Call for BLOCKED/CAUTION/APPROVAL decisions (not SAFE — keep DB-free per §1.2).
Non-blocking: open DB, log, close. Errors ignored.

## 3. Cleanup/Prune Wiring
In handleApproval() after approval logic, call:
  _ = database.CleanupExpired()
  _ = database.PruneEvents(10000)

In runner.go after logEvent(), if database != nil:
  _ = database.CleanupExpired()
  _ = database.PruneEvents(10000)

## 4. Config LogLevel Wiring
Load config in runHookInternal():
  cfg, _ := config.LoadConfig(config.ConfigPath())
Apply log level via events.SetLogLevel(cfg.LogLevel).
Add SetLogLevel(level string) to events/logger.go.

Acceptance: All 4 concerns addressed. Tests for disable marker. make lint clean.

```validation
{"content_check": {"file": "internal/adapters/hook.go", "pattern": "IsDisabled"}, "tests": "go test -race ./..."}
```

title: Add MCP matcher to fuse install claude
